title: Tail-latency thesis boundary
detail: The paper claims improved p99 latency under bursty multi-tenant traffic, not universal throughput gains.

title: NSDI over SIGCOMM
detail: Deployment realism and operational evidence are more central than protocol novelty for this project.

title: Tail metrics are mandatory
detail: Always report p99, worst-case behavior, and throughput tradeoff together. Average latency alone is not decision-quality evidence here.

title: Workload realism risk
detail: Do not claim deployment relevance unless at least one production-like trace is included in the main paper.

title: Synthetic-only traces are insufficient
detail: Prior internal reviews rejected deployment claims when experiments used only synthetic burst models.
